refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability ≥ 0.999 (99.9%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the bidirectional command-and-telemetry link is operational to total evaluation time.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-010 addresses a critical operational requirement: remote operators must reliably command and receive feedback from equipment. The 0.999 threshold (approximately 43 minutes downtime per month) is a standard for safety-critical teleoperation. Refinement clarifies measurement scope (bidirectional link), evaluation period (customer-defined), and the quantitative target.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous monitoring of link state transitions (up/down) during the evaluation period; automated logging of outage duration and root cause; statistical analysis of availability = (total_time - total_downtime) / total_time.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-010.1: The teleoperation system shall establish and maintain a bidirectional command link from tele-operator to vehicle.
FR-NGHE-010.2: The teleoperation system shall establish and maintain a bidirectional telemetry link from vehicle to tele-operator.
FR-NGHE-010.3: The teleoperation system shall detect link failures and initiate failover or safe shutdown within defined time bounds.
FR-NGHE-010.4: The teleoperation system shall log and report link availability metrics to support verification against the 0.999 target.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Redundant network paths (primary + backup) with automatic failover; dual-modem architecture; geographic diversity of network providers.
DP-B: Single high-reliability network path with aggressive error correction and retransmission; QoS prioritization; local caching of critical commands.
DP-C: Hybrid approach: primary path with redundant backup; local autonomy mode when link is lost; operator-in-the-loop recovery protocol.
DP-D: Mesh network topology with multi-hop relay capability; distributed link monitoring; dynamic rerouting on path failure.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid approach combining a primary teleoperation path with a secondary backup path, local autonomy mode for safe operation during link loss, and operator-in-the-loop recovery protocol. This balances cost, complexity, and safety: redundancy improves availability; local autonomy ensures safety during transient outages; operator recovery reduces false alarms.

potential_sources: DP-C couples teleoperation link availability to vehicle autonomy capability (FR for safe autonomous operation must be satisfied independently).
DP-C couples to network infrastructure selection (choice of primary/backup providers affects achievable availability).
DP-C couples to operator training (recovery protocol effectiveness depends on operator skill).
coupling_check.notes: Couplings are manageable: autonomy is a separate FR tree; network selection is a design decision within Teleoperation module scope; operator training is a stakeholder responsibility. No circular dependencies detected.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Customer-defined evaluation period is ≥ 30 days (sufficient statistical confidence for 0.999 target).
Assumption: Primary and backup network paths have independent failure modes (geographic/provider diversity is achievable).
Risk: If primary and backup paths share a common failure point (e.g., single site gateway), redundancy is ineffective.
Risk: Local autonomy mode may not be acceptable for all worksite safety plans; requires explicit safety case review.
Risk: Operator recovery protocol adds latency; if link recovery is slow, equipment may enter unintended safe state, reducing productivity.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly specify: (1) independence criteria for primary/backup paths (e.g., different carriers, geographic separation ≥ 10 km); (2) local autonomy mode trigger conditions and safe state definition; (3) operator recovery time budget (e.g., ≤ 5 seconds to restore full control).

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Owns the bidirectional command and telemetry link between tele-operator and vehicle, including primary/backup network paths, failover logic, local autonomy mode, and link monitoring/reporting. Interfaces with Vehicle (for command execution and telemetry collection), Safety Services (for safe state enforcement), and external Network Link (for connectivity).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator commands (movement, tool control, mode selection) from tele-operator console; routed through primary or backup network path.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits vehicle state (position, orientation, sensor readings, link quality metrics) to tele-operator console; routed through primary or backup network path.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints from Safety Services (e.g., geofence, speed limits); sends link failure alerts to trigger safe shutdown or local autonomy mode.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Abstracts primary and backup network connectivity (cellular, satellite, or hybrid); Teleoperation module manages failover between paths.

module_containment_scores.notes: Teleoperation is the natural owner of link availability logic. Primary/backup path management, failover, and link monitoring are core to this module. Minimal diffusion: only SafetyAlert interface requires coordination with Safety Services.

Vehicle module owns command execution and telemetry collection, but NOT link availability. Assigning link availability to Vehicle would scatter network/failover logic across vehicle firmware, creating high diffusion and poor separation of concerns.

Safety Services owns safe state enforcement and hazard mitigation, but NOT network link management. Link availability is a prerequisite for safety, not a safety function itself. Assigning to Safety Services would conflate network engineering with safety logic.
NGHE Solution is the system-level container; assigning link availability here would bypass architectural decomposition and leave implementation scattered across sub-modules.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Teleoperation module contains all primary logic for achieving 0.999 link availability: dual-path network abstraction, failover state machine, link quality monitoring, and recovery protocol. Vehicle and Safety Services are consumers of the link (via Tasking and SafetyAlert interfaces), not owners.
containment_risks: Risk: If Network Link interface is not properly abstracted, Teleoperation module may become tightly coupled to specific network hardware/providers, reducing reusability.

Risk: Local autonomy mode logic may require tight coupling to Vehicle control logic; if Vehicle module does not expose a clean 'autonomous mode' interface, Teleoperation will have high diffusion into Vehicle.

Risk: Link monitoring and metrics reporting may require access to low-level network diagnostics; if these are not exposed via Network Link interface, Teleoperation will need to reach into network stack, creating diffusion.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure Network Link interface exposes: (1) link state (up/down/degraded); (2) failover capability (switch between primary/backup); (3) link quality metrics (latency, packet loss, signal strength). Ensure Vehicle module exposes: (1) autonomous mode enable/disable; (2) safe state entry/exit. These clean interfaces will minimize diffusion and keep Teleoperation as the clear owner of link availability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid redundancy + local autonomy + operator recovery) directly addresses FR-NGHE-010 (achieve 0.999 link availability). Redundant paths reduce outage frequency; local autonomy ensures safety during transient failures; operator recovery reduces false alarms. No logical gaps detected. DP-C is feasible with current technology (dual modems, failover logic, autonomous mode are proven in industry).

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: If Vehicle module does not expose a clean autonomous mode interface, Teleoperation will need to directly manipulate Vehicle control logic, creating tight coupling.
Diffusion risk: If Network Link abstraction is incomplete (e.g., low-level modem control exposed), Teleoperation will scatter failover logic across multiple modules.

Diffusion risk: Link monitoring and metrics reporting may require access to Safety Services for safe state coordination; if SafetyAlert interface is not bidirectional, Teleoperation will have to poll Safety Services, creating latency and coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation is the correct owner (containment_score 0.95). Diffusion is low if interfaces (Network Link, Vehicle autonomous mode, SafetyAlert) are well-defined. Recommend interface specification review before detailed design.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. Link availability is measurable via continuous monitoring of link state and outage logs. Verification can be automated: deploy link monitor in Teleoperation module, log state transitions, compute availability = (total_time - total_downtime) / total_time. Acceptance test: run evaluation period (customer-defined, ≥ 30 days), confirm availability ≥ 0.999.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. Teleoperation module is allocated the requirement to achieve 0.999 link availability. Sub-requirements (FR-NGHE-010.1 through 010.4) are allocated to Teleoperation sub-components (primary path, backup path, failover logic, monitoring). Traceability is straightforward.

recommended_revisions: Specify Network Link interface contract: link state, failover capability, quality metrics.
Specify Vehicle autonomous mode interface: enable/disable, safe state definition.
Specify SafetyAlert interface as bidirectional: Teleoperation sends link failure alerts; Safety Services sends safe state constraints.
Define evaluation period and measurement methodology in acceptance criteria (e.g., 30-day continuous operation, automated link state logging).
Add risk mitigation for common failure points: require primary/backup path independence analysis (different carriers, geographic separation).

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-010 is not atomic; it decomposes into four sub-FRs (command link, telemetry link, failure detection, metrics reporting). DP-C is sound and well-matched to Teleoperation module. Containment is high (0.95) with manageable diffusion risks. However, before proceeding to detailed design, interface specifications must be clarified to minimize diffusion. Recommend one more decomposition level to refine sub-FRs and allocate them to Teleoperation sub-components (primary path manager, backup path manager, failover state machine, link monitor).
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-010.1: The teleoperation system shall establish and maintain a bidirectional command link from tele-operator to vehicle with latency ≤ [customer-defined] ms.
FR-NGHE-010.2: The teleoperation system shall establish and maintain a bidirectional telemetry link from vehicle to tele-operator with update rate ≥ [customer-defined] Hz.
FR-NGHE-010.3: The teleoperation system shall detect link failures (primary path down) and initiate failover to backup path within [customer-defined] seconds.
FR-NGHE-010.4: The teleoperation system shall log link state transitions and compute availability metrics for verification against 0.999 target.

revise.why: DP-C is sound, but requires refinement on: (1) primary/backup path independence criteria (to ensure redundancy is effective); (2) local autonomy mode trigger conditions and safe state definition (to ensure safety case is clear); (3) operator recovery time budget (to ensure productivity impact is acceptable). Recommend DP revision to include these specifics before implementation.

assumptions: Customer-defined evaluation period is ≥ 30 days (sufficient statistical confidence).
Primary and backup network paths can be made independent (different carriers, geographic separation).
Vehicle module can support autonomous mode (safe operation without operator input).
Operator recovery protocol is acceptable to Safety Manager (does not violate worksite safety plan).
Network Link interface can be abstracted to hide low-level modem/carrier details.
open_risks: Common failure point risk: if primary/backup paths share a single gateway or network provider, redundancy is ineffective. Mitigation: require independence analysis and geographic diversity.
Safety case risk: local autonomy mode may not be acceptable for all worksites. Mitigation: conduct safety case review with Safety Manager before implementation.
Operator training risk: recovery protocol effectiveness depends on operator skill. Mitigation: define training requirements and validation procedures.
Interface diffusion risk: if Vehicle, Safety Services, or Network Link interfaces are not well-defined, Teleoperation will have high diffusion. Mitigation: specify interfaces before detailed design.
evidence_needed: Network provider independence analysis (confirm primary/backup paths have different failure modes).
Safety case for local autonomy mode (confirm safe state definition and transition logic).
Operator recovery protocol specification (define trigger conditions, time budget, training requirements).
Interface specifications (Network Link, Vehicle autonomous mode, SafetyAlert).
Evaluation plan (measurement methodology, evaluation period, acceptance criteria).
